title: Verify Parcel Lifecycle Message Contents At Induction And Sorter Entry
summary: Use the training source to verify the expected contents of the first and second parcel lifecycle messages described before sorter induction and after sorter entry. The source states that the first message contains tracking ID, dimensions, and package type, and the second message contains tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.
when_to_use: Use when a user needs a source-backed reference check of what data elements should appear in the first parcel lifecycle message before sorter induction and the second parcel lifecycle message when the parcel is on the sorter.
not_for: Do not use to infer additional message fields beyond those stated in the source.
Do not use to infer message transport, screen names, or system interfaces not stated in the source.
Do not use to determine message timing beyond the lifecycle positions explicitly described by the source.
Do not use as troubleshooting logic for why a message failed unless separately supported by source evidence.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review available parcel lifecycle message details or evidence and compare observed fields to the source-described first and second message contents.

safety_notes: This is a reference-style verification procedure based on training material.
Do not infer additional controls, destinations, or message behavior beyond what the source states.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to parcel lifecycle message details or evidence
Training slide describing first and second message contents

access_required: Access to parcel lifecycle message details or evidence
system_preconditions: The user has an observed or recorded parcel lifecycle message to compare against the source-described field lists.
external_references: Training slide describing first and second message contents

visual_references.description: Lifecycle of a parcel training slide showing the first and second Chat messages and their documented field contents.

steps.title: Locate the first parcel lifecycle message before sorter induction
steps.purpose: Identify the source-described first lifecycle message at the correct point in parcel flow.
steps.instruction: Use the lifecycle slide to locate the parcel stage before sorter induction and identify the first message described by the source.
steps.expected_result: The first lifecycle message is identified at the pre-induction stage of parcel flow.
steps.healthy_condition: The source view clearly shows the first message associated with parcels before sorter induction.
steps.failure_condition: The first message cannot be confidently located in the source material or cannot be tied to the pre-induction stage.

actions.label: Locate first message on lifecycle slide
actions.exact_action: Locate the parcel lifecycle stage before sorter induction and identify the first message described by the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The lifecycle slide area describing the first Chat message before sorter induction.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the first message cannot be matched to the source-described lifecycle stage.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Lifecycle of a parcel slide: first Chat message contains tracking ID, dimensions, and package type; second Chat message contains tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.

steps.title: Verify first message field contents
steps.purpose: Confirm the first message contains the exact data elements documented by the source.
steps.instruction: Verify that the first message contains tracking ID, dimensions, and package type.
steps.expected_result: The first message matches the source-described field list.
steps.healthy_condition: Tracking ID, dimensions, and package type are present in the first message.
steps.failure_condition: One or more of tracking ID, dimensions, or package type is missing, or unexpected interpretation is required to make the message fit the source.

actions.label: Check first message fields
actions.exact_action: Verify that the first message contains tracking ID, dimensions, and package type.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The first Chat message field list showing tracking ID, dimensions, and package type.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if message contents cannot be matched to the source-described first message structure.
Stop if additional fields must be inferred because the source does not define them.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Lifecycle of a parcel slide: first Chat message contains tracking ID, dimensions, and package type; second Chat message contains tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.
The slide describes a first Chat message containing tracking ID, dimensions, and package type.

steps.title: Locate the second parcel lifecycle message on the sorter
steps.purpose: Identify the source-described second lifecycle message at the point where the parcel is on the sorter.
steps.instruction: Locate the parcel lifecycle stage where the parcel is on the sorter and identify the second message described by the source.
steps.expected_result: The second lifecycle message is identified at the on-sorter stage.
steps.healthy_condition: The source view clearly shows the second message associated with the parcel being on the sorter.
steps.failure_condition: The second message cannot be confidently located in the source material or cannot be tied to the on-sorter stage.

actions.label: Locate second message on lifecycle slide
actions.exact_action: Locate the parcel lifecycle stage where the parcel is on the sorter and identify the second message described by the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The lifecycle slide area describing the second Chat message after sorter entry.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the second message cannot be matched to the source-described lifecycle stage.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The slide describes a second Chat message when the parcel is on the sorter.

steps.title: Verify second message field contents
steps.purpose: Confirm the second message contains the exact data elements documented by the source.
steps.instruction: Verify that the second message contains tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.
steps.expected_result: The second message matches the source-described field list.
steps.healthy_condition: Tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data are present in the second message.
steps.failure_condition: One or more of tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, or potential destination data is missing, or unexpected interpretation is required to make the message fit the source.

actions.label: Check second message fields
actions.exact_action: Verify that the second message contains tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if message contents cannot be matched to the source-described second message structure.

steps.title: Compare observed contents and record mismatches
steps.purpose: Document whether observed message contents align with the source-described field lists.
steps.instruction: Compare the observed message contents to the source-described field lists and record any missing or unexpected data elements.
steps.expected_result: A clear comparison result is recorded for both the first and second messages.
steps.healthy_condition: Observed message contents align with the documented field lists for the applicable lifecycle stage.
steps.failure_condition: Observed message contents do not align with the documented field lists, or the message cannot be mapped to either source-described structure.

actions.label: Compare and record message contents
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed message contents to the source-described field lists and record any missing or unexpected data elements.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The documented first and second message field lists used as the comparison baseline.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if message contents cannot be matched to the source-described first or second message structure.
Escalate if the comparison requires inferring additional message fields or timing beyond what the source states.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Lifecycle of a parcel slide: first Chat message contains tracking ID, dimensions, and package type; second Chat message contains tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.

success_criteria: The first message is verified against the source-described fields: tracking ID, dimensions, and package type.
The second message is verified against the source-described fields: tracking ID, barcode data, dimensional data, and potential destination data.
Any missing or unexpected data elements are recorded without adding unsupported assumptions.
healthy_conditions: The observed first message matches the documented first message field list.
The observed second message matches the documented second message field list.
The lifecycle stage of each message can be matched to the source description.
failure_conditions: The observed message cannot be matched to the source-described first or second message structure.
Required documented fields are missing from the observed message contents.
The review would require inferring additional fields, timing, or behavior not stated in the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if message contents cannot be matched to the source-described first or second message structure.
Escalate if the available evidence is insufficient to determine whether the message corresponds to the pre-induction or on-sorter stage.
Do not infer additional message fields or message timing beyond what the source states.

missing_details: The source does not identify a specific application screen, interface name, or message viewer for performing the verification.
